subject: Ela
title: ELA Test - Day 03
description: Focus on verb tenses, multiple-meaning words, chronological sequencing, and sentence corrections.

questions.q: Which verb correctly completes the sentence in the past tense? 'Yesterday, Clara ___ a beautiful picture of a coral reef during art class.'
questions.explanation: 'Drew' is the irregular past tense form of the verb 'draw'.

questions.q: Identify the helping verb in the following sentence: 'The brave astronauts have traveled deep into space.'
questions.explanation: 'Have' is the helping (auxiliary) verb that works with the main action verb 'traveled' to form the present perfect tense.

questions.q: Choose the correct form of the verb to ensure subject-verb agreement: 'The pack of wolves ___ loudly through the dark forest.'
questions.explanation: The subject 'pack' is a singular collective noun, so it requires the singular verb form 'howls'.

questions.q: Which definition fits the word **bark** as it is used in this sentence? 'We noticed that the rough bark of the ancient oak tree was peeling away.'
options: The loud sound made by a guard dog.
To shout out orders in an angry voice.
The tough protective outer layer of a tree trunk.
A small sailing ship used long ago.
questions.answer: The tough protective outer layer of a tree trunk.
questions.explanation: The context clues 'ancient oak tree' show that 'bark' refers to the tree's outer covering.

questions.q: What does the prefix **pre-** mean in words like 'preheat' or 'preview'?
questions.explanation: The prefix 'pre-' means before, so 'preheat' means to heat up beforehand.

questions.q: What happened immediately after the pioneer family crossed the muddy river?
questions.passage: In May of 1845, a pioneer family packed their canvas wagon to head west. First, they traveled across wide prairies for three long weeks. Next, they arrived at a rushing, muddy river where they had to carefully float their wagon across. Immediately after reaching the other side safely, they set up camp and cooked a hot meal. Finally, they began the steep climb up into the rocky mountains before winter arrived.
options: They climbed into the rocky mountains.
They set up camp and cooked a hot meal.
questions.answer: They set up camp and cooked a hot meal.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Immediately after reaching the other side safely, they set up camp and cooked a hot meal.'

questions.q: Which word from the passage signals the final step in the pioneer family's journey?
questions.passage: In May of 1845, a pioneer family packed their canvas wagon to head west. First, they traveled across wide prairies for three long weeks. Next, they arrived at a rushing, muddy river where they had to carefully float their wagon across. Immediately after reaching the other side safely, they set up camp and cooked a hot meal. Finally, they began the steep climb up into the rocky mountains before winter arrived.
questions.explanation: The transition word 'Finally' explicitly introduces the last step in their journey sequence.

questions.q: Which sentence has a mistake in its grammar or usage?
options: He doesn't want any homework tonight.
The children were playing outside in the rain.
She seen that movie three times already.
We went to the grocery store to buy apples.
questions.answer: She seen that movie three times already.
questions.explanation: 'Seen' cannot stand alone as a main verb without a helping verb like 'has' or 'had'. It should be 'She has seen' or 'She saw'.

questions.q: Choose the sentence that is written correctly without any errors.
options: Me and my brother love to play basketball.
My brother and I love to play basketball.
My brother and me love to play basketball.
I and my brother love to play basketball.
questions.answer: My brother and I love to play basketball.
questions.explanation: When used as a compound subject, 'My brother and I' is grammatically correct, placing the first-person pronoun last and using the subject form 'I'.

questions.q: What is the function of the coordinating conjunction in this sentence? 'Sam wanted to buy the video game, **but** he did not have enough money saved.'
options: To show a choice between two things.
To add more information to a thought.
To show a contrast or conflict.
To explain the reason for something.
questions.answer: To show a contrast or conflict.
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'but' is used to connect two contrasting or conflicting ideas.
